name: LLM Annotation Is Acceptable Only After Benchmarking Against Expert Human Ratings
statement: Nebhi et al. benchmark candidate LLM annotators against expert human grammatical-accuracy ratings before using a majority-vote ensemble to annotate synthetic writing responses for scoring-model development.
population_or_context: Adaptive English writing assessment for EFSET.
quantitative_note: The source reports Cohen kappa values of 0.86 for LLaMA 3.3 70B, 0.87 for GPT-4o, 0.78 for Nova Pro, and 0.89 for the selected ensemble against a human-annotated gold standard.
caveats: Human benchmarking is based on approximately 200 item-response pairs.
LLM majority vote is not equivalent to independent human scoring of all generated responses.
The response type is proficiency-aligned English writing.
evaluation_method: Cohen kappa is used to select LLM annotators against expert human ratings before majority-vote annotation of synthetic response pairs.
reported_metrics: Cohen kappa by candidate model and ensemble; synthetic response-pair counts.
limitations: Does not remove the need to document label source and human benchmark size.
Does not prove synthetic labels are valid across all writing prompts or scoring rubrics.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the expert-human gold standard, model-selection kappa table, majority-vote procedure, and synthetic response-pair annotation.
